Helpful Tips:
1. Before using the plates, make sure to remove the protective plastic cover from the front.
2. To avoid ruining the designs on your nails, wet a cotton ball with pure acetone and hold it with tweezers.
3. Use the acetone-soaked cotton ball to wipe off any nail polish from the plates and stamper.
4. Ensure that the plate is completely dry before applying more polish.
5. For stamping, use a thick and highly pigmented polish.
6. Work quickly! If you take too long, the polish may dry on the plate or stamper, making it difficult to transfer.
7. If the polish doesn't transfer, try using a different type of polish, as some dry quicker than others.
8. When applying top coat, be gentle to avoid smearing your design.

Argan Oil - 4 oz. Our 100% Pure Moroccan Argan Oil is free from additives and preservatives. It is packaged in a high-quality amber glass bottle, ensuring its freshness. The Argan Tree, scientifically known as Argania spinosa from the Sapotaceae family, is native to Morocco and can only be found there. These trees have an impressive lifespan of 150-200 years. For centuries, Berber women in Morocco have kept the secret of traditional cosmetic products made from Argania spinosa, using them to protect their skin, hair, and nails from the harsh and dry weather conditions. Argan oil is considered to be one of the rarest oils in the world. To create cosmetic-grade Argan oil, the freshly harvested kernels from the Argan tree's fruit are pressed and filtered through an organic cotton sieve, resulting in the extraction of the aromatic oil. This oil is easily absorbed by the skin and is commonly used for treating dermatologic conditions such as acne. The method used to extract Argan oil is also utilized in Aromatherapy. Although Argan oil has been widely used in the Aromatherapy industry in Europe for a long time, it remains relatively unknown in the United States. Argan oil contains nearly twice as much vitamin E or a-tocopherol as olive oil, making it a highly beneficial ingredient. Additionally, it is rich in linoleic acids. The anti-aging properties of Argan oil, attributed to its high levels of vitamin E and saponins that soften the skin, have been extensively studied. Tocopherols play a crucial role in human health due to their antioxidant properties and ability to combat free radicals.

Here's how to use them:
1. Take out one strip of lashes from the case at a time.
2. Apply a line of eyelash glue along the entire length of the lash strip.
3. Wait for the glue to become tacky (around 30-50 seconds) rather than wet.
4. Starting from the middle, place the lash strip on your natural lash line, ensuring it is centered.

Optional: Apply mascara if desired.

Helpful Tips:
- Before using the plates, make sure to remove the protective plastic cover from the front.
- To avoid smudging the designs on your nails, wet a cotton ball with pure acetone and hold it with tweezers while cleaning.
- Use the acetone-soaked cotton ball to wipe off any nail polish from the plates and stamper.
- Ensure that the plate is completely dry before applying more polish. For stamping, use a thick and highly pigmented polish.
- Work quickly! If you take too long, the polish may dry on the plate or stamper, and you won't be able to transfer it onto your nails.
- If the polish doesn't transfer even when working quickly, try using a different type of polish. Some polishes dry faster than others.
- When applying top coat, be gentle to avoid smearing your design.

To determine the expiration date, you can refer to the lot code. The lot code consists of six to seven digits and begins with either 0, 1, 2, or 3. By reading the lot code, you can identify various information about the product. The first three digits represent the day of the year the product was manufactured, also known as the Julian Date. The following two digits indicate the year, while the last two digits represent an internal batch number used for identification purposes. For instance, if the lot code is 0400901, it means that the product was made on February 9, 2009, and belongs to Batch Number 1.
